formulas.name: The Act of Settlement
formulas.formula: An act passed by the British Parliament in 1701 to ensure the Protestant succession to the throne.
formulas.notes: This act led to the establishment of British rule in India.

formulas.name: The Regulating Act of 1773
formulas.formula: An act passed by the British Parliament to reform the East India Company and its administrative structure.
formulas.notes: This act led to the establishment of a Supreme Court in Calcutta and the appointment of a Governor-General.

formulas.name: The Charter Act of 1833
formulas.formula: An act passed by the British Parliament to reform the East India Company and extend British rule in India.
formulas.notes: This act led to the appointment of the Governor-General as the Chief Executive of British India.

formulas.name: The Doctrine of Lapse
formulas.formula: A policy introduced by Lord Dalhousie to annex Indian states that did not have a male heir.
formulas.notes: This policy led to the annexation of many Indian states and the expansion of British rule in India.

formulas.name: The Doctrine of Discovery
formulas.formula: A policy introduced by the British to justify the annexation of Indian territories.
formulas.notes: This policy was used to justify the annexation of Indian territories and the expansion of British rule in India.
